'''''UFC 274: Oliveira vs. Gaethje''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on May 7, 2022, at the [[Footprint Center]] in [[Phoenix, Arizona]], United States.<ref name="Phoenix">{{Cite web|url=https://www.azcentral.com/story/sports/mma/2022/03/06/ufc-274-set-phoenix-may-7-saffords-justin-gaethje-main-event/9387613002/|title=UFC 274 headed to Phoenix on May 7 at Footprint Center|publisher=azcentral.com|author=José M. Romero|date=2022-03-05|access-date=2022-03-06}}</ref>

==Background==
The event was originally linked to [[Rio de Janeiro]], [[Brazil]], but the promotion eventually decided to move the event from that location.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://ge.globo.com/combate/noticia/ultimate-muda-planos-e-tira-ufc-274-do-rio-de-janeiro.ghtml|title=Ultimate changes plans and removes UFC 274 from Rio de Janeiro|publisher=ge.globo.com|author=Raphael Marinho|date=2022-01-25|access-date=2022-01-25}} {{in lang|pt}}</ref> It took place in [[Phoenix, Arizona]]. It was the city's first event since [[UFC on ESPN: Ngannou vs. Velasquez]] in February 2019.<ref name="Phoenix" />

A [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Glover Teixeira]] and former [[Rizin Fighting Federation#Light Heavyweight Championship|Rizin Light Heavyweight Champion]] [[Jiří Procházka]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/33123401/sources-glover-teixeira-jiri-prochazka-light-heavyweight-title-set-ufc-274-may|title=Sources: Glover Teixeira-Jiri Prochazka light heavyweight title bout set for UFC 274 in May|work=espn.com|author=Brett Okamoto|date=2022-01-22|access-date=2022-01-22}}</ref> However, the bout was pushed back to [[UFC 275]] for undisclosed reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/2/27/22953018/glover-teixeira-vs-jiri-prochazka-championship-fight-at-ufc-274-rescheduled-for-ufc-275|title=Glover Teixeira vs. Jiri Prochazka championship fight at UFC 274 rescheduled for UFC 275|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2022-02-27|access-date=2022-02-27}}</ref>

A [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Charles Oliveira]] and former interim champion [[Justin Gaethje]] (also former [[WSOF Lightweight Championship|WSOF Lightweight Champion]]) headlined the event.<ref name="LWtitle">{{Cite web|date=2022-01-26|title=Justin Gaethje set to challenge Charles Oliveira for lightweight title at UFC 274|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/01/ufc-news-justin-gaethje-set-to-challenge-charles-oliveira-for-lightweight-title-at-ufc-274|access-date=2022-01-27|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matthew Wells and Danny Segura}}</ref> At the weigh-ins, Oliveira weighed in at 155.5 pounds, half a pound over the title limit. As a result, upon commencement of the fight, Oliveira was stripped of the title and only Gaethje was eligible to win it.<ref name="Weighin">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/05/ufc-274-weigh-in-results-and-live-video-stream-noon-et|title=UFC 274 weigh-in results: Charles Oliveira misses weight, stripped of title|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2022-05-06|access-date=2022-05-06}}</ref> 

A [[UFC Women's Strawweight Championship]] rematch between current two-time champion [[Rose Namajunas]] and former champion (also former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Strawweight Championship|Invicta FC Strawweight Champion]]) [[Carla Esparza]] took place at the event.<ref name="SWtitle">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/3/16/22982204/rose-namajunas-vs-carla-esparza-2-set-for-ufc-274-co-main-event|title= Rose Namajunas vs. Carla Esparza 2 set for UFC 274 co-main event|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Shaun Al-Shatti|date=2022-03-16|access-date=2022-03-16}}</ref> The pairing previously met 7 and a half years prior at [[The Ultimate Fighter: A Champion Will Be Crowned Finale]] for the inaugural championship, which Esparza won via third round submission.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2014/12/12/7377363/tuf-20-finale-results-esparza-vs-namajunas|title= TUF 20 Finale Results: Esparza vs. Namajunas|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Bryan Tucker|date=2014-12-12|access-date=2022-03-16}}</ref> 

A lightweight bout between [[Joe Lauzon]] and former lightweight title challenger [[Donald Cerrone]] was scheduled for [[UFC on ESPN: Font vs. Vera]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/01/ufc-news-donald-cerrone-vs-joe-lauzon-cowboy-april-30|title=UFC targeting Donald Cerrone vs. Joe Lauzon for April 30 event|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2022-01-28|access-date=2022-01-28}}</ref> However, the bout was removed from this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Donald-Cerrone-vs-Joe-Lauzon-Shifted-to-UFC-274-on-May-7-184747|title=Donald Cerrone vs. Joe Lauzon shifted to UFC 274 on May 7|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Tristen Critchfield|date=2022-02-15|access-date=2022-02-15}}</ref> In turn, the bout was cancelled on the day of the event when Cerrone suffered from food poisoning.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/5/7/23061633/donald-cowboy-cerrone-out-of-ufc-274-due-to-illness-fight-against-joe-lauzon-pulled-from-card|title=Donald 'Cowboy' Cerrone out of UFC 274 due to illness, fight against Joe Lauzon pulled from card|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2022-05-07|access-date=2022-05-07}}</ref>

[[Amanda Ribas]] and former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Atomweight Championship|Invicta FC Atomweight Champion]] [[Michelle Waterson]] were expected to meet at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/03/ufc-274-amanda-ribas-vs-michelle-waterson-rebooked-may-7|title=Amanda Ribas vs. Michelle Waterson rebooked for UFC 274 on May 7|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2022-03-01|access-date=2022-03-01}}</ref> They were originally expected to meet at [[UFC 257]], but Waterson pulled out of the bout due to undisclosed reasons.<ref name="WatersonRibas">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/12/8/22164091/michelle-waterson-out-of-upcoming-fight-against-amanda-ribas-at-ufc-257|title=Michelle Waterson out of upcoming fight against Amanda Ribas at UFC 257|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin and Guilherme Cruz|date=2020-12-08|access-date=2020-12-08}}</ref> They were then rescheduled for [[UFC Fight Night: Blaydes vs. Daukaus]], but an injury forced Waterson out of the pairing once again.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/3/1/22955761/amanda-ribas-michelle-waterson-postponed-ufc-274|title=Amanda Ribas vs. Michelle Waterson rescheduled for UFC 274|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2021-03-01|access-date=2022-03-01}}</ref> Waterson announced in early March that her injury could potentially be "career ending" and would not compete at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/03/ufc-news-michelle-waterson-says-amanda-ribas-fight-off-career-ending-injury|title=Michelle Waterson says Amanda Ribas fight off because of potentially 'career-ending' injury|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Simon Samano|date=2022-03-03|access-date=2022-03-04}}</ref>

A lightweight bout between [[Michael Johnson (fighter)|Michael Johnson]] and [[Alan Patrick (fighter)|Alan Patrick]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mma.uno/?p=191470|title=Alan Patrick vs Michael Johnson added to UFC 274|publisher=mma.uno|author=Steve Duncan|date=2022-02-25|access-date=2022-04-25}} {{in lang|es}}</ref> However, the bout was pushed back one week to [[UFC on ESPN: Błachowicz vs. Rakić]] for undisclosed reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmarocks.pl/mma-news/michael-johnson-otrzyma-kolejna-szanse-w-oktagonie-ufc|title=Michael Johnson will get another chance in the UFC octagon|publisher=mmarocks.pl|author=Michał Tkaczuk|date=2022-04-25|access-date=2022-04-25}} {{in lang|pl}}</ref>

At the weigh-ins, [[Norma Dumont]] weighed in at 146.5 pounds, half a pound over the women's featherweight non-title fight limit. Her bout proceeded at catchweight and she was fined 30% of her individual purse, which went to her opponent [[The Ultimate Fighter: Heavy Hitters]] women's featherweight winner [[Macy Chiasson]].<ref name="Weighin" />

It was announced during the event's broadcast that former [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship|UFC Light Heavyweight]] and [[UFC Heavyweight Championship|Heavyweight Champion]] [[Daniel Cormier]] will be inducted into the [[UFC Hall of Fame]] at this year's class.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/05/ufc-news-daniel-cormier-to-be-inducted-into-the-ufc-hall-of-fame|title=Former two-division champion Daniel Cormier to be inducted into the UFC Hall of Fame|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura|date=2022-05-08|access-date=2022-05-09}}</ref>

This event had five split decision bouts tying [[UFC 222]] for the most split decision bouts in UFC history.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2019/01/ufc-in-2018-stats-streaks-skids-record-setters-champions-titles-history|title=UFC in 2018: A ridiculously robust look at the stats, streaks, skids and record-setters|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2019-01-09|access-date=2022-06-14}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/lists/ufc-274-bonuses-michael-chandlers-knockout-of-the-year-contender-among-winners|title=UFC 274 bonuses: Michael Chandler's knockout of the year contender among winners|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2022-05-08|access-date=2022-05-09}}</ref>
* '''Fight of the Night: Brandon Royval vs. Matt Schnell'''
* '''Performance of the Night: Michael Chandler''' and '''André Fialho'''

The following fighters received [[Crypto.com]] "Fan Bonus of the Night" awards paid in [[bitcoin]] of US$30,000 for first place, US$20,000 for second place, and US$10,000 for third place.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bloodyelbow.com/2022/05/08/ufc-274-post-fight-bonuses-chandler-plants-ferguson-with-koty-candidate/|title=UFC 274 post-fight bonuses: Chandler plants Ferguson with KOTY candidate|publisher=bloodyelbow.com|author=Stephie Haynes|date=2022-05-08|access-date=2025-07-05}}</ref>
* '''First Place:''' Rose Namajunas
* '''Second Place:''' Michael Chandler
* '''Third Place:''' Charles Oliveira
